title: External Data Source
description: Configuration for AI Fabrix ExternalDataSource entities. Includes metadata schema, data dimensions, transformation mappings, OpenAPI/MCP exposure, execution logic, and sync behavior.

metadata.name: External Data Source Configuration Schema
metadata.description: JSON schema for validating ExternalDataSource configuration files
metadata.author: AI Fabrix Team

changes: Initial schema for External Data Sources
Added fieldMappings DSL with transformation expressions
Added metadataSchema validation
Added exposed fields configuration for MCP/OpenAPI
Added ABAC accessFields validation
Added OpenAPI and MCP operation configurations
Added sync configuration and validation rules
Added execution.engine with CIP and Python execution modes
Introduced CIP definition ($defs.cipDefinition and step types)
Added capabilities for list/get/create/update/delete
Extended fieldMappings with semantic metadata for AI
Added exposure profiles for different AI/analytics views
Added data quality rules, indexing, and AI context hints
Added config.abac section with crossSystemSql and crossSystemJson properties
Added support for unified filter model (JSON and SQL formats) in ABAC cross-system configuration
Updated CIP filter expression to support both SQL and JSON formats
BREAKING: Renamed fieldMappings.accessFields (array) to fieldMappings.dimensions (object)
BREAKING: Renamed fieldMappings.fields to fieldMappings.attributes
BREAKING: Renamed entityKey to entityType
BREAKING: Renamed exposed.fields to exposed.attributes
Added indexed property to attribute definitions for database index creation
Updated descriptions to reflect dimension-first data model approach
Added error semantics (onError object) to all CIP step types with error classification, retry, compensation, and failPipeline
Added idempotency configuration to CIP definition for execution-level replay guarantees
Added lineage configuration to field mappings for field-level explainability and compliance
Added contract versioning configuration to datasource root for CI/CD safety and agent stability
capabilities: preferred format is array ["list","get",...].

BREAKING: Added required primaryKey (array of normalized attribute names). Used for get/update/delete and table indexing. Migration: add primaryKey array (e.g. ["id"] or ["externalId"]) to each datasource config.
BREAKING: Consolidated freeze baseline into 2.4.0 on feature branch before merge.
BREAKING: Removed metadataSchema.foreignKey, top-level references, and relationContract.
BREAKING: Removed fieldMappings.dimensions and datasource config.abac* contract surfaces.
BREAKING: Added root contract gate for entityType='none' (orchestration/chaining only).
BREAKING: Removed indexing section from datasource root; index/filter are metadataSchema-driven.
BREAKING: Simplified fieldMappings.attributes to expression-only normalization contract.
Updated expression contract to strict roots: raw.*, fk.*, dimension.* with FK traversal support.
Added top-level dimensions and strict foreignKeys[] contract (required name/fields/targetDatasource).
Simplified sync contract to mode/schedule/batchSize.
Hardened metadataSchema index/filter scalar-only constraints.
Applied naming guardrails for metadata fields (camelCase) and FK names (camelCase).
Added datetime representation guidance: type=string + format=date-time with UTC/native DB timestamp mapping.
Allowed contract/config.extensions for entityType=none orchestration datasources.
Added actor/operator strict defaults in dimensionBinding (eq for displayName/email/userId, in for groups/roles).
CIP execution.cip.operations allows custom operation keys matching the same pattern as capabilities[].
recordStorage/documentStorage: dimensions are no longer JSON Schema-required; validator warns when missing or empty.
dimensionBinding type=fk: via remains required; actor is optional (warn when omitted).
dimensions object may be empty at schema level; recommend ≥1 binding via application validation warning.
testPayload: unknown top-level keys rejected by application validator (strict contract beyond JSON Schema).

cipStepFetch: fetch.openapiRef and fetch.operation (source=datasource) use the same naming pattern as capabilities[] and execution.cip.operations keys (^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$), not a fixed CRUD enum. Standard names list/get/create/update/delete remain valid; custom keys must match openapi.operations.<name> or the target datasource CIP operation.
recordStorage/documentStorage: metadataSchema.properties.externalId required (type=string, index=true) as global join identity (346.rules §10.1; plans SC-025/SC-029).
foreignKeys.targetFields: when omitted, resolution defaults to target externalId (not primaryKey); composite primaryKey must not be used as explicit cross-datasource join key (SC-026/SC-028).
Application validator enforces externalId field mapping and FK join semantics in lockstep with JSON Schema.

entityType.description: Entity type identifier. Defines storage semantics and behavior: 'documentStorage' (creates document storage service with vector-storage), 'vectorStore' (external vector storage system), 'recordStorage' (creates record-based system with metadata sync and access rights), 'messageService' (message service for notifications - Slack, Teams, Email), 'none' (uses external system data directly or connects other data sources). Required field. Canonical values are camelCase.

resourceType.description: Resource type for the datasource. Represents business entity classification. Common values: customer, contact, person, document, deal, record. Any lowercase alphanumeric string with hyphens is allowed.

primaryKey.description: Normalized field names that uniquely identify a record (used for get/update/delete and table indexing). Each element must exist in metadataSchema and be index=true.

labelKey.description: Normalized field names used for display and dimension labels. Each element must exist in metadataSchema and be scalar with index=true or filter=true.

foreignKeys.description: Foreign key definitions referencing other datasources. Defines relational integrity and join capability.

dimensions.description: Canonical dimension bindings for this datasource. Keys must exist in Dimension Catalog when catalog validation runs. For recordStorage/documentStorage, at least one binding is recommended (application validator may warn if missing or empty).

fieldMappings.description: Normalization rules only (raw/fk traversal to normalized attributes). For storage datasources, attribute keys must correspond to metadataSchema fields (builder/runtime validation).

attributes.description: Key = normalized attribute name. Value = transformation configuration.

expression.description: Pipe-based DSL expression. Allowed roots: raw.<path>, fk.<fk>.metadata.<field>, fk.<fk>.dimension.<dimension>[.label], dimension.<dimension>[.label]. FK traversal in DSL is one-hop only.

description.description: Technical description of the normalized field.

semantic.description: Semantic metadata for AI agents and schema generation.

description.description: Business-level description of the field and how it is used.

synonyms.description: Optional list of synonyms used in natural language (e.g. 'opportunity', 'sales-case').

exposed.description: Defines public API exposure contract. v2.4.0 freeze requires exposed.schema as canonical response shape.

schema.description: Canonical public response contract. Keys define output shape; values are expressions using metadata./fk./dimension paths.

omit.description: Fields that exist in normalized metadata but must NEVER be exposed by MCP/OpenAPI (e.g., secrets, internal IDs). Overrides 'fields'.

filterable.description: Fields that may be used as deterministic filters in exposed APIs. Must reference materialized fields (`index=true` or `filter=true`).

required.description: Fields that profile contracts require to be present.

readonly.description: Fields exposed via MCP/OpenAPI but cannot be modified via write operations.

description.description: Human-readable description of the exposure model for this data source.

profiles.description: Named exposure profiles mapping profile keys to deterministic field contracts.

schedule.description: Cron schedule for sync execution. Supports 5-field cron syntax or @hourly/@daily/@weekly/@monthly.

openapi.description: OpenAPI-driven connector configuration. Only includes endpoints selected during wizard onboarding. To add more endpoints, run the wizard again.

documentKey.description: OpenAPI document key used to resolve selected operations. Warning-only in this story when unresolved at validation time.

operations.description: Selected operations from the OpenAPI document. Only endpoints chosen during wizard onboarding are included.

autoRbac.description: When true and no explicit operation security scopes are configured, the runtime resolves required RBAC permission as '<datasource-key>:<operation>' for each operation (autoRbac resolution). Explicit operation-level scopes (openapi.operations.*.security/permissions) take precedence and disable autoRbac for that operation.

validation.description: Advanced validation rules applied before saving normalized metadata.

quality.description: Data quality rules applied after mapping but before persistence and AI exposure.

rejectIf.description: List of conditions that cause a record to be rejected. For lessThan: missing field is treated as reject. For greaterThan: missing field is not rejected. See quality docs for operator semantics.

context.description: Natural-language and semantic hints for AI agents.

synonyms.description: Terms users might use in natural language to refer to this datasource or its primary entity.

naturalLanguageHints.description: Free-form hints for LLMs on how to interpret or use this datasource.

documentStorage.description: Document storage configuration (optional). Schema is canonical under type/document-storage.json.

messageService.description: Message service configuration (optional). Schema is canonical under type/message-service.json.

testPayload.description: Execution-aware test payload configuration for unit and integration testing. Only the listed properties are allowed at this level; nested shapes for payloadTemplate/expectedResult/fk fixtures are validated strictly by the application validator where required.

primaryKey.description: Optional key/value payload for primary-key focused operation tests.

fk.description: Optional FK fixture payload used by expression and join tests.

actors.description: Optional actor fixture list for ABAC/runtime tests.

payloadTemplate.description: Sample payload matching the expected API response structure. Used for testing field mappings and metadata schema validation.

expectedResult.description: Expected normalized result after field mapping transformations (optional, for validation)

execution.description: Execution engine configuration for this datasource (CIP, Python, or datasource-chaining). CIP is the native declarative mode.

engine.description: Execution engine. 'cip' for declarative pipelines, 'python' for custom handlers, 'datasource' for datasource-chained CIP execution.

extensions.description: Vendor extensions namespace for controlled schema evolution.

metadataSchemaNode.description: Strict supported subset of JSON Schema for metadataSchema.

index.description: Storage/index hint for metadata persistence. True marks this field as index-eligible.

filter.description: Filterability hint for metadata-driven query planning.

format.description: For datetime semantics use type='string' with format='date-time' (UTC at runtime).

name.description: Mandatory foreign key name (camelCase) for debugging, UI, and generated contracts (must be unique per datasource).

fields.description: Local normalized fields forming the foreign key. Each field must exist in metadataSchema and have index=true (builder/runtime enforced).

targetFields.description: Target fields joined by this FK. If omitted, defaults to target metadataSchema externalId (["externalId"]) for cross-datasource identity (not target primaryKey). Explicit multi-field lists must not mirror the target composite primaryKey as the join contract.

required.description: If true, missing or invalid reference causes validation failure

exposedSchemaNode.description: Recursive exposed.schema node. Object values create nested API objects; string values are expression leaves.

openapiOperation.description: Selected OpenAPI operation contract with explicit security metadata.

security.description: OpenAPI security requirements. Supports object/list/string forms consumed by RBAC coverage validation.

cipRuntimeContext.description: Runtime context requirements for CIP execution.

pythonExecution.description: Python-based execution config for advanced/custom use cases.

entrypoint.description: Python callable in module notation, e.g. 'handlers.hubspot_deal.list_records'.

codeRef.description: Optional reference to external code bundle identifier or embedded block key.

cipLineage.description: Optional lineage metadata for a CIP operation or step.

cipDefinition.description: Composable Integration Pipeline (CIP) definition for this datasource.

inputType.description: Logical input type of the pipeline. Reserved for future extensions.

outputType.description: Logical output type of the pipeline.

operations.description: Logical operations implemented for this datasource. Standard names: list, get, create, update, delete. Additional keys are allowed when they match the capability naming pattern (same as capabilities[] items).

method.description: Optional canonical HTTP method for this operation contract.

path.description: Optional canonical route/path for this operation contract.

safe.description: Optional safety hint for operation execution.

cipStep.description: One step in the CIP pipeline.

fetch.description: Fetch raw records from an upstream system.

source.description: If 'openapi', refer to openapi.operations.*. If 'http', use explicit method/path. If 'datasource', execute another datasource operation.

openapiRef.description: Key under openapi.operations (standard CRUD: list, get, create, update, delete, or any custom name matching capabilities[] / execution.cip.operations naming).

operationId.description: OpenAPI operationId to call when source='openapi'. Optional when openapiRef is used.

operation.description: Target datasource operation when source='datasource' (standard CRUD or custom CIP operation key on the target). Defaults to list.

parameters.description: Optional operation parameters forwarded to datasource execution when source='datasource'.

query.description: Static query parameters. ABAC/runtime can still override or append at runtime.

bodyTemplate.description: Optional static request body or template for POST/PUT/PATCH fetches.

expectedItemsPath.description: Optional response shape guard. When set, runtime validates this path exists and is non-null in fetch response.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers for the request (compatibility contract; runtime support may depend on executor path).

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

paginate.description: Pagination strategy applied to results from the preceding fetch.

cursorField.description: Field in the response payload containing the cursor value (for 'cursor' strategy).

cursorParam.description: Name of the query parameter used to send the cursor.

pageParam.description: Name of the query parameter used to send the page number (for 'page' strategy).

pageSizeParam.description: Name of the query parameter used to send the page size.

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to fetch.

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

map.description: Transform raw API records into the normalized metadata model.

inputPath.description: JSONPath-like expression to iterate over records in the upstream payload.

useFieldMappings.description: If true, apply the datasource.fieldMappings.attributes expressions; no inline mapping needed.

inline.description: Optional inline mapping overrides { normalizedField -> expression }.

additionalProperties.description: Pipe-based DSL expression overriding fieldMappings expression.

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

abac.description: ABAC filtering behavior for this step.

expression.description: JMESPath expression evaluated after ABAC filtering.

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

output.description: Finalize the CIP pipeline and emit records.

limit.description: Optional soft limit for records returned by list operation; sync runner can override.

includeConfidence.description: If true, the pipeline will compute a confidence score per record based on quality checks, mapping completeness, and source hints.

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

pythonInline.description: Sandboxed inline Python transformation, operating on the current record batch.

code.description: Small, pure function snippet; no imports, no IO, no network. Runtime must enforce sandboxing.

stepId.description: Unique identifier for this step within the operation

backoff.description: Backoff strategy: exponential (2^attempt), linear (attempt * base), fixed (constant)

maxDelaySeconds.description: Maximum delay between retries in seconds

cipCompensationConfig.description: Compensation (rollback) configuration for error handling. Current runtime compensation execution is focused on fetch/map step flows.

stepRef.description: Reference to a previously executed step by stepId to execute as compensation

cipOnErrorConfig.description: Enhanced error handling configuration for CIP steps.

class.description: Error classification: transient (retryable), permanent (non-retryable), validation (data validation failure), auth (authentication/authorization failure)

failPipeline.description: Whether step failure should fail the entire pipeline (false allows partial success)

scope.description: Idempotency scope: datasource (same key within datasource) or system (same key across all datasources)

ttlSeconds.description: Time to live for idempotency records in seconds (default: 86400 = 24 hours)

enforcement.description: Enforcement mode: strict (return previous result, no execution) or best-effort (warn + continue)

transformationRule.description: Transformation rule applied to a field.

rule.description: Human-readable rule description (e.g., 'open → OPEN, closed → CLOSED')

fieldMappingLineage.description: Lineage metadata for a field mapping.

sourceFields.description: Source field paths that contribute to this mapped field (e.g., ['raw.fields.state'])

transformations.description: List of transformations applied to source fields

confidence.description: Confidence score for this mapping (0.0-1.0). Default: 1.0 for deterministic transformations.

backwardCompatible.description: Whether this version is backward compatible with previous versions

breakingChanges.description: List of breaking change types: removeField, changeType, changeSemantics

sunsetDate.description: Date when this contract version will be sunset (YYYY-MM-DD)

replacedBy.description: Contract name/version that replaces this deprecated version

name.description: Contract identifier (e.g., 'jira.issue', separate from datasource key)

status.description: Contract status: stable (production-ready), deprecated (being phased out), experimental (testing)

compatibility.description: Compatibility information for this contract version

triggerPathsHash.description: SHA256 hash of triggerPaths payload (64-char hex). Used to detect structural changes. Optional; Dataplane computes when absent.
